title: Create your first alert rule - handling and save

blocks.content:
Welcome to the final learning path for creating your first alert rule. Handling options decide what happens when a query returns no data or errors, notifications reach the right people through the right channel, and saving makes your rule active.

In this path, you'll set no-data and error handling, select a contact point, add an annotation, and save your rule.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Configure no-data and error handling
- Select a contact point for notifications
- Add a summary annotation to your alert
- Save and activate your alert rule

## Before you begin

Before you finish your alert rule, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- An alert rule with a query, condition, labels, and evaluation behavior configured. To set those up, complete the [query](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/alerting-first-rule-query/), [condition](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/alerting-first-rule-condition/), and [labels and evaluation](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/alerting-first-rule-labels/) paths.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

After you save your first rule, explore silences to suppress notifications during planned maintenance.
